raw_user_messages:
<codex_delegation>
  <source_thread_id>019eaf56-91db-7c40-b740-7435313502d3</source_thread_id>
  <input>Use the evaluator-supplied workspace D:\project\.codex-eval-full\CALIBRATION-20260613-03\workspaces\E12\state_matrix. For each independent project under scenarios/, recover the compact state, build the deterministic ADR metadata projection, validate replacement relations and PLAN references, and report blocking conflicts. Read only the bodies needed for the current task.</input>
</codex_delegation>
commentary: I’m using the project-workflow skill because this is a multi-project recovery and validation task. I’ll read its operating instructions first, then inspect only the scenario files needed to derive and verify each state.

The task is recovery plus path/reference validation, so I’m loading the workflow’s verification rules. After that I’ll inventory scenario boundaries and likely state artifacts without opening unrelated bodies.

There are 12 independent fixtures. I’ll now read every compact `PLAN.md` first, then extract only ADR headings and the six permitted metadata fields from each ledger; bodies will be loaded solely where a PLAN reference requires them.

The headings expose the expected edge cases, but the metadata syntax is not simple colon lines. I’m narrowing the extraction to lines containing only the permitted field names and ADR anchors, which keeps ledger bodies out of context while revealing the deterministic projection.

The projection now has clear governance values (`已确认` / `已替代`) and explicit replacement edges. I’m running structural checks next, then loading only the H2 bodies explicitly named by PLAN; for scenario 12 that also includes the referenced PRD “Retention Rules” section.

That extraction attempt produced no visible output because the nested PowerShell invocation swallowed the pipeline, not because the files were empty. I’m rerunning the same read-only parsing directly in the active shell and will use its results for the final conflict matrix.
final_response:
**Result**

Read all 12 compact PLANs, projected ADR metadata from headings and permitted metadata only, then loaded only PLAN-referenced bodies. No files were changed.

Projection format: `id | title | governance_state | replaces_ids | replaced_by_id | body_anchor`

```text
01
ADR-001 | Legacy queue       | 已替代 | []                | ADR-003 | #adr-001-legacy-queue
ADR-002 | Legacy retry       | 已替代 | []                | ADR-003 | #adr-002-legacy-retry
ADR-003 | Unified delivery   | 已替代 | [ADR-001,ADR-002] | ADR-004 | #adr-003-unified-delivery
ADR-004 | Current delivery   | 已确认 | [ADR-003]         | null    | #adr-004-current-delivery
ADR-005 | Metrics            | 已确认 | []                | null    | #adr-005-metrics
ADR-006 | Alerting           | 已确认 | []                | null    | #adr-006-alerting

02
ADR-001 | Old deploy path    | 已替代 | []        | ADR-002 | #adr-001-old-deploy-path
ADR-002 | New deploy path    | 已确认 | []        | null    | #adr-002-new-deploy-path

03
ADR-001 | Old storage        | 已替代 | []        | ADR-003 | #adr-001-old-storage
ADR-002 | Alternate storage  | 已替代 | []        | ADR-003 | #adr-002-alternate-storage
ADR-003 | Current storage    | 已确认 | [ADR-002] | null    | #adr-003-current-storage

04
ADR-001 | Old API            | 已替代 | []        | ADR-002 | #adr-001-old-api
ADR-002 | Current API        | 已确认 | [ADR-001] | null    | #adr-002-current-api

05
ADR-001 | Email notifications | 已确认 | [] | null | #adr-001-email-notifications
ADR-001 | Push notifications  | 已确认 | [] | null | #adr-001-push-notifications

06
ADR-001 | Cache policy       | null   | []        | null    | #adr-001-cache-policy

07
ADR-001 | Current index      | 已确认 | [ADR-999] | null    | #adr-001-current-index

08
ADR-001 | Export format      | 已替代 | [ADR-001] | ADR-001 | #adr-001-export-format

09
ADR-001 | Scheduler A        | 已替代 | [ADR-002] | ADR-002 | #adr-001-scheduler-a
ADR-002 | Scheduler B        | 已替代 | [ADR-001] | ADR-001 | #adr-002-scheduler-b

10
ADR-001 | Old authentication | 已确认 | []        | ADR-002 | #adr-001-old-authentication
ADR-002 | Current authentication | 已确认 | [ADR-001] | null | #adr-002-current-authentication

11
ADR-001 | Logging            | 已确认 | []        | null    | #adr-001-logging
ADR-002 | Metrics            | 已确认 | []        | null    | #adr-002-metrics
ADR-003 | Old deployment     | 已替代 | []        | ADR-004 | #adr-003-old-deployment
ADR-004 | Current deployment | 已确认 | [ADR-003] | null    | #adr-004-current-deployment

12
ADR-001 | Export format      | 已确认 | []        | null    | #adr-001-export-format
ADR-002 | Retention period   | 已确认 | []        | null    | #adr-002-retention-period
```

**Blocking Conflicts**

| Scenario | Result |
|---|---|
| 01 | Valid. Active state: ADR-004, ADR-005, ADR-006. |
| 02 | Blocked: unilateral edge; ADR-001 names ADR-002, but ADR-002 does not replace ADR-001. |
| 03 | Blocked: ADR-001 points to ADR-003, but ADR-003 only replaces ADR-002. |
| 04 | Blocked: duplicate `ADR-001 replaced-by ADR-002` declaration. |
| 05 | Blocked: duplicate `ADR-001`; active notification decision is ambiguous. |
| 06 | Blocked: governance state is missing. |
| 07 | Blocked: replacement target `ADR-999` does not exist. |
| 08 | Blocked: self-replacement/self-cycle. |
| 09 | Blocked: two-node replacement cycle; no terminal current decision. |
| 10 | Blocked: ADR-001 is marked confirmed despite being replaced by ADR-002. |
| 11 | Blocked: PLAN calls ADR-003 active although superseded, references missing ADR-999, and omits active ADR-002/ADR-004. |
| 12 | Valid. Preserve public export format; retain completed records 30 days; existing maintenance removes expired records. |

Scenario 12’s unrelated `audits/ARCHIVE.md` was not read. Relevant sources: [scenarios](/D:/project/.codex-eval-full/CALIBRATION-20260613-03/workspaces/E12/state_matrix/scenarios), [scenario 12 PLAN](/D:/project/.codex-eval-full/CALIBRATION-20260613-03/workspaces/E12/state_matrix/scenarios/12-selective-loading/docs/PLAN.md), [PRD](/D:/project/.codex-eval-full/CALIBRATION-20260613-03/workspaces/E12/state_matrix/scenarios/12-selective-loading/docs/PRD.md).

output.preview:
Exit code: 0
Wall time: 2.8 seconds
Output:
---
name: codex-project-workflow
description: Structure and govern Codex work for new or ambiguous projects, multi-step implementation, professional research, solution comparison, architecture and planning, third-party downloads, high-risk changes, project recovery, or requests for a rigorous workflow. Also use when Codex must create, generate, export, or save a deliverable through an existing product or application, even if the request looks like one step, and for ordinary tasks when research, confirmation, implementation-path verification, recovery, or coordination may matter. Do not use for simple questions or explicit low-risk one-step edits or commands.
---

# Codex Project Workflow

## Route

1. Inspect first. Keep goal, constraints, and completion test. Ask only blocking decisions.
2. From the repo root containing this skill, run `python .agents/skills/codex-project-workflow/scripts/read_reference.py NAME "Execution Rules" "Output Requirements"`. Use `research` for unstable evidence/options; `governance` for third-party assets, installs, execution, private networking, approvals, agents, or durable changes; `verification` for writes, product-generated outputs, recovery, production/core paths, or path proof. Load each triggered protocol only.
3. Never list headings, scan skill directories, or read references directly. Report helper failure; it waives no gate.
4. Agents suit three streams, conflicting evidence, or high failure cost. Load governance, propose count/roles, ownership, parallel/main work, benefit, cost, risks, and fallback, then ask. Only explicit acceptance starts them; safe unrelated work may continue.

## Safeguards

- Never invent execution, sources, tests, or verification.
- Before writes map entry, state, owners, and bypasses. Preserve ownership; freeze an evidence-bound contract for production routing, persistence/schema/migration, architecture, core capability, alternate output, explicit path proof,

arguments.command: python '.agents/skills/codex-project-workflow/scripts/read_reference.py' verification 'Execution Rules' 'Output Requirements'

output.preview:
Exit code: 0
Wall time: 2.1 seconds
Output:
## Execution Rules

- Create a formal implementation contract before the first relevant write when any trigger applies: production entry or routing changes; state authority, persistence, schema, or migration changes; architecture boundary or ownership changes; new, copied, or replacement core capability; a direct alternate final-output path; explicit user path proof; or high-impact commitment.
- For a generated final delivery that also writes product state or persistence, save the frozen contract to `docs/IMPLEMENTATION_CONTRACT.md` before any delivery or state write unless the project defines another contract path.
- Before freezing that contract, identify the user-invocable entry from root-level CLI, UI, API, or registration evidence and record its exact invocation. An internal function is never the product entry merely because it reaches the desired core path.
- Freeze the contract from independent evidence before writing. If the source, version, entry, state update, save path, or ownership cannot be proven, stop as implementation-path unverified; do not borrow a sibling, neighboring fixture, helper, or post-hoc contract.
- File count, module count, tests plus code, and mechanical cross-file edits do not trigger a formal contract by themselves. When every trigger is false, keep a one-to-three-line internal path constraint.
- Bind contract claims to independent project evidence such as confirmed docs, registration points, callers, runtime entry points, or state definitions. Preserve later contract differences.
- Trace the existing user entry. Create, generate, export, or save requests never authorize changing entry wiring, routing, core code, or tests; that requires a separate explicit repair request. Internal calls cannot replace the entry. If it bypasses the core path, stop before contract, delivery, state, or product-code writes and report implementation-path unverified.
- Verify result and implementation pat
